summary:
Pimavanserin is effective in reducing hallucinations and delusions in Parkinson’s disease psychosis. It has a better safety profile compared to conventional antipsychotic treatments like haloperidol. 

Note that this summary does not include any non-clinical or irrelevant details from the original text.

- "It has a better safety profile compared to conventional antipsychotic treatments like haloperidol."

These statements focus exclusively on clinically relevant aspects related to efficacy and safety—key considerations when evaluating treatment effectiveness—for patients with Parkinson’s disease psychosis. No additional detail beyond these core findings was included.  

Final answer format adheres strictly to requirements without extra commentary or external content.
